danger_signals.visual_form: square-shaped enemies approaching from front and behind
danger_signals.meaning: imminent collision threat; enemy fire incoming

danger_signals.visual_form: force shield visual reduction or flickering
danger_signals.meaning: player vulnerability increasing; one hit away from destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: colored square-shaped items appearing from defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: opportunity to strengthen weapon, shield, or gain lives

safety_signals.visual_form: yellow power-up destruction effect clearing all visible enemies
safety_signals.meaning: temporary respite; brief window of safety
death resilience increased; risk tolerance expanded

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player ship design remains consistent; no faction-specific environmental markers documented

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: generic enemy sprites (largely non-descript mechanical/organic designs)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: enemies spawn continuously from screen edges; no persistent territorial markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: sparse enemy density; manageable projectile patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies move in predictable formations; slower approach speed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: moderate enemy density increase; denser bullet patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies employ more complex approach vectors; increased fire rate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: heavy screen coverage of projectiles; rapid enemy spawning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies exhibit aggressive clustering; minimal safe zones on screen

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player weapon visuals intensify (wider spread, larger projectiles); shield glow effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can sustain more aggressive forward positioning; reduced evasion necessity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player projectile spread diminishes; shield glow fades or absent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player forced into defensive posture; requires constant evasion

environmental_storytelling.example: pink power-ups = main weapon upgrade; green = side weapons; blue = shield recovery; orange = extra life; yellow = screen clear. Visual encoding removes need for text; player learns through play iteration.
enemy formation density and projectile patterns increase across five-stage progression. Visual screen fill gradually escalates, communicating escalating threat without narrative exposition.
red power-up visually switches between Darius I and Darius II shot styles. Player immediately recognizes weapon state change through projectile visual patterns.
orange power-ups grant extra Silver Hawk ships. Multiple ship losses visible through HUD tracking. Loss of final ship = loss condition communicated through absence.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: moves toward player from front or behind; drops power-up loot on defeat; exhibits no avoidance behavior
projectile spread widens; weapon fire rate increases; evasion capability improves through visual feedback
single-stream projectiles; slow fire rate; high evasion demand signals vulnerability
glow effect around player ship; absorbs one additional hit before destruction
glow effect absent; next hit triggers ship destruction; visual absence signals critical vulnerability

notes: Darius Twin employs pure mechanical manifestation rather than narrative or political world-state communication. All danger/safety signals, faction identity, and world-state changes are expressed through visual game object presentation and behavioral response systems. The game communicates exclusively through symbol and mechanic; no explicit narrative or faction lore elements documented. Power-up color-coding is the primary linguistic system. Enemy design lacks faction identity markers, suggesting monolithic threat rather than multi-faction conflict.
